description: NBA outcomes are nicknames ['Knicks','Spurs']; codes SA→sas, NY→nyk; 00:30Z dates to prior ET day; home/away index resolved by name.

headlines.description: — Jalen Brunson and the Comeback Knicks did it again. And now they're the Champion Knicks.
headlines.shortLinkText: The Comeback Knicks are the Champion Knicks. Brunson scores 45, and New York tops Spurs for title

video.headline: The drought is over! The Knicks win their first NBA title since 1973

tracking.trackingName: NBA_One-Play (The drought is over! The Knicks win their first NBA title since 1973) 2026/06/14 ESHEET

highlights.description: The drought is over! The Knicks win their first NBA title since 1973

espn.name: New York Knicks at San Antonio Spurs

polymarket.description:
In the upcoming NBA game, scheduled for June 13 at 8:30PM ET:
If the Knicks win, the market will resolve to "Knicks".
If the Spurs win, the market will resolve to "Spurs".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.
The result will be determined based on the final score including any overtime periods.

eventMetadata.context_description: The 2026 NBA Finals matchup between the New York Knicks and San Antonio Spurs featured contrasting regular-season records, with the Spurs finishing 62-20 atop the Western Conference standings behind Victor Wembanyama and the Knicks at 53-29 as the East's third seed. Recent series developments included the Knicks building a 3-1 lead through strong defensive execution and comebacks, capped by a 94-90 Game 5 clincher in San Antonio where Jalen Brunson scored 45 points. Injury reports highlighted Mitchell Robinson's availability despite a hand issue, while the Spurs maintained a relatively clean bill of health. Home-court dynamics, playoff experience edges, and momentum shifts from earlier rounds shaped trader consensus around series outcomes and individual game probabilities.
